Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Long Overlooked as Crucial to Life, Fungi Start to Get Their Due 23 upvotes, 0 comments
  2. Printf-Tac-Toe 106 upvotes, 11 comments
  3. Dolphin Progress Release 2603 229 upvotes, 38 comments
  4. High fidelity font synthesis for CJK languages 42 upvotes, 4 comments
  5. Show HN: Understudy – Teach a desktop agent by demonstrating a task once 15 upvotes, 0 comments
  6. Italian prosecutors seek trial for Amazon, 4 execs in alleged $1.4B tax evasion 115 upvotes, 21 comments
  7. The Met Releases High-Def 3D Scans of 140 Famous Art Objects 93 upvotes, 21 comments
  8. Avoiding Trigonometry (2013) 179 upvotes, 48 comments
  9. The Cost of Indirection in Rust 45 upvotes, 9 comments
  10. Reversing memory loss via gut-brain communication 38 upvotes, 7 comments
  11. Malus – Clean Room as a Service 541 upvotes, 197 comments
  12. 3D-Knitting: The Ultimate Guide 194 upvotes, 69 comments
  13. Full Spectrum and Infrared Photography 11 upvotes, 1 comments
  14. Apple's MacBook Neo makes repairs easier and cheaper than other MacBooks 25 upvotes, 6 comments
  15. Show HN: OneCLI – Vault for AI Agents in Rust 42 upvotes, 14 comments
  16. Emacs internals: Tagged pointers vs. C++ std:variant and LLVM (Part 3) 48 upvotes, 17 comments
  17. Lf-lean: The frontier of verified software engineering 6 upvotes, 0 comments
  18. WolfIP: Lightweight TCP/IP stack with no dynamic memory allocations 6 upvotes, 0 comments
  19. Asia rolls out 4-day weeks, WFH to solve fuel crisis caused by Iran war 218 upvotes, 118 comments
  20. US banks' exposure to private credit hits $300B (2025) 179 upvotes, 101 comments
  21. Bubble Sorted Amen Break 37 upvotes, 15 comments
  22. Claude now creates interactive charts, diagrams and visualizations 71 upvotes, 40 comments
  23. The Road Not Taken: A World Where IPv4 Evolved 10 upvotes, 3 comments
  24. Kotlin creator's new language: a formal way to talk to LLMs instead of English 176 upvotes, 146 comments
  25. Show HN: LogClaw – Open-source AI SRE that auto-creates tickets from logs 13 upvotes, 6 comments
  26. Show HN: Web-Based ANSI Art Viewer 3 upvotes, 0 comments
  27. Suburban school district uses license plate readers to verify student residency 151 upvotes, 176 comments
  28. ATMs didn't kill bank Teller jobs, but the iPhone did 141 upvotes, 178 comments
  29. Scrt: A CLI secret manager for developers, sysadmins and DevOps 4 upvotes, 1 comments
  30. Converge (YC S23) Is Hiring a Founding Platform Engineer (NYC, Onsite) 0 upvotes, 0 comments

Last generated 2:00am, 13 March 2026. Source here.
